lever.name: contiguous-row B-superpanel GEMM traversal
lever.description: Within each packed-k block, traverse bounded row superpanels and B column superpanels so adjacent NR outputs for one MR row tile are accumulated and stored contiguously before moving to the next row tile.

after.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 RCH_WORKER=vmi1293453 RCH_WORKERS=vmi1293453 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR RCH_DAEMON_WAIT_RESPONSE_TIMEOUT_SECS=180 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-rch-fjlax-7vhjh-after cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'linalg/matmul_2d_2048x2048x2048_f64' --sample-size 10 --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 3 --noplot

proof.golden_command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 RCH_WORKER=vmi1293453 RCH_WORKERS=vmi1293453 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR RCH_DAEMON_WAIT_RESPONSE_TIMEOUT_SECS=180 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-rch-fjlax-7vhjh-proof cargo test -p fj-lax --lib matmul_2d -- --nocapture
isomorphism: Thread row-block ownership and output partitioning are unchanged.
A and B packing formats are unchanged.
For each output element, accumulation still runs in ascending pc blocks and ascending l within each block; only independent output-tile visitation order changes.
Floating-point product/add order per output element is unchanged.
No ordering/tie-breaking, RNG, dtype promotion, error, or assertion surface changes.
